**[On the Evolutionary, Bio-Psychological Foundations of the Human-Animal Relationship].**. Prax Kinderpsychol Kinderpsychiatr.. PMID: 38051058. DOI: 10.13109/prkk.2023.72.8.666. Ever since, people live in contact with nature and animals, even in relatively non-utilitarian ways. Erich Fromm and Edward Wilson termed this human universal "Biophilia". But why different species can live together in asocial way, is explained by a "common social toolbox" of neural, psychological and physiologicalmechanisms, which evolved over phylogeny.Major components of this toolbox are found in the vertebrate brain, which evolved over the past 600 million years in asuccession of key innov
